evals.prompt: How do I write a log statement in Go that includes the current user ID and request ID automatically?
evals.expected_output: Use log.W(ctx) instead of package-level log functions. log.W(ctx) automatically extracts X-Request-ID and X-User-ID from context and adds them to every log line. Pass key-value pairs as arguments: log.W(ctx).Infow('User created', 'username', username). Never add request ID or user ID fields manually.

evals.prompt: I want to format my log message as 'Failed for user alice'. Is this correct?
evals.expected_output: No. Never format values into the message string. Use key-value pairs instead: log.W(ctx).Errorw('Failed for user', 'user', uid). This keeps the message constant and indexable by log systems. Also avoid zap typed fields (zap.String, zap.Error) with the Sugar API — they break the API contract.

evals.prompt: Which layers of my Go service should log errors?
evals.expected_output: Only the biz layer logs internal technical failures. Handler layer does not log — core.HandleJSONRequest handles it. Store layer does not log — just returns errno.ErrDBRead.WithMessage(err.Error()). Server init logs startup/shutdown events. The rule: log raw technical error where you have context, return sanitized errno sentinel.

should_not_trigger: How do I define custom error sentinels in the errno package?
